perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that Cano shall be identified as the lead sponsor, by having a thirty second lead-in promotion at the beginning of each episode; a thirty second trailer promotion at the end of each episode; and two thirty second commercials during each episode.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that Cano may be identified as a sponsor, potentially through a lead-in promotion, trailer, or commercials, at the Company's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity. The original text specifies Cano as the 'lead sponsor' with guaranteed placements. The modified text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' making the sponsorship benefits discretionary and undermines Cano's guaranteed placement as the lead sponsor. The word 'sponsor' instead of 'lead sponsor' also makes the term vague. This contradicts section 19 which states: This Agreement supersedes any and all other agreements, either oral or in writing, between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this contract, and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the parties with respect to the subject matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement supersedes any and all other agreements, either oral or in writing, between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this contract, and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the parties with respect to the subject matter. Each party to this contract acknowledges that no representations, inducements, promises, or agreements, orally or otherwise, have been made by any party, or anyone acting on behalf of any party, that are 2 not set forth in this contract, and that no agreement, statement, or promise not contained in this contract shall be valid or binding.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees Cano specific sponsorship placements ('lead sponsor,' 'thirty second lead-in,' 'thirty second trailer,' 'two thirty second commercials'). The changed text replaces this with discretionary sponsorship opportunities ('may be identified as a sponsor, potentially through a lead-in promotion, trailer, or commercials, at the Company's discretion'). This undermines the previously agreed-upon guaranteed benefits. The contradicted text emphasizes that the agreement contains all covenants and agreements, and that no other agreements outside of the contract are binding. The change transforms a guaranteed aspect of the agreement into a discretionary one, which is a contradiction based on the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction and creates uncertainty and conflicting obligations within the contract itself.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall utilize the already produced lead-in, commercials and trailers.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall endeavor to utilize the already produced lead-in, commercials and trailers, provided they align with the Company's current marketing strategy.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Company's use of pre-produced materials. The changed text introduces the phrase 'endeavor to utilize' and a condition based on the 'Company's current marketing strategy.' This creates uncertainty by allowing the Company to potentially reject the materials, thus changing what was a mandatory obligation to something discretionary. This now contradicts to Section 6, were it says 'Cano has already provided the Company with voice over lead-in(s), 30 second commercials and trailers of a quality satisfactory to the Company and content that meets the Company's minimum standards.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Provision of Lead in Trailer and Commercials. Cano has already provided the Company with voice over lead-in(s), 30 second commercials and trailers of a quality satisfactory to the Company and content that meets the Company's minimum standards. The Company shall endeavor to utilize the already produced lead-in, commercials and trailers, provided they align with the Company's current marketing strategy.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the use of pre-produced materials. The changed text makes the use of these materials conditional based on the 'Company's current marketing strategy.' This condition allows the Company to reject the materials even though 'Cano has already provided the Company with voice over lead-in(s), 30 second commercials and trailers of a quality satisfactory to the Company and content that meets the Company's minimum standards.' The change, therefore, contradicts the prior agreement and established quality standards, creating an ambiguity regarding the Company's obligation to use the provided materials.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement, contingent upon Sponsor's satisfaction with the Show's viewership metrics, the Company may be entitled to compensation up to $150,000.00, subject to Sponsor's final approval after each installment period.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely alters the payment terms. Originally, the Company was 'entitled' to a fixed compensation paid in installments. The modified text introduces contingencies: 'Sponsor's satisfaction,' 'Show's viewership metrics,' and 'Sponsor's final approval.' The 'shall' is replaced with 'may' and adds 'up to' to introduce ambiguity about the ammount. This now makes the obligation to pay conditional and variable, contradicting the original fixed payment schedule and guaranteed ammount. This contradicts section 1 which states: The Company shall produce no less than forty (40) original episodes of the Show per year, since this obligation has not changed. The section 15 is now contradictin the section 1 since the section 1's obligagion remains but the payment is no longer secured.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Required Production. The Company shall produce no less than forty (40) original episodes of the Show per year;
perturbation.justification: The original text guaranteed compensation of $150,000.00, paid in installments. The changed text makes the compensation contingent on 'Sponsor's satisfaction' and 'Show's viewership metrics,' and subject to 'Sponsor's final approval,' introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'The Company shall produce no less than forty (40) original episodes of the Show per year,' as the company is still obligated to produce the episodes but is no longer guaranteed payment for doing so. The change removes the 'entitlement' to compensation, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the obligation remains but the payment for that obligation is no longer secured.
